THE FINAL WEIGHT OF THE FRAME, $ W_f $, CAN BE EXPRESSED AS $ W_f = W \times (1 - 0.02)^n $, WHERE $ W $ IS THE BASE WEIGHT OF THE FRAME AND $ N $ IS THE NUMBER OF ADVANCED COMPONENTS ADDED. THIS FORMULA ACCOUNTS FOR THE 2% WEIGHT REDUCTION PER COMPONENT.

TO ACHIEVE A TOTAL REDUCTION OF ENERGY EXPENDITURE BY 25%, THE FUNCTION $ E(n) = 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n $ MUST EQUAL OR EXCEED 0.25. SOLVING $ 1 - (1 - 0.02)^n \geq 0.25 $ GIVES $ (1 - 0.02)^n \leq 0.75 $. TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES, $ n \ln(0.98) \leq \ln(0.75) $. SOLVING FOR $ N $, WE FIND $ N \geq \ln(0.75)/\ln(0.98) \approx 13.51 $. SINCE $ N $ MUST BE AN INTEGER, THE MINIMUM NUMBER OF COMPONENTS REQUIRED IS 14.

THE CLOCK TICKS FORWARD AS THE TARGET IS APPROACHING. THE CLOCK SHOWS THE URGENT NEED TO MEET THE GOAL. THE CLOCK REMINDS THAT EVERY SECOND COUNTS TOWARD ACHIEVING THE DESIRED PERFORMANCE IMPROVEMENT.
